title: I Can Be Wealthy and Kind
subtitle: An affirmation for releasing the belief that significant wealth is incompatible with being a good person
excerpt: A short daily affirmation for the fear that wealth changes character. For use when financial growth triggers the worry that having more makes you someone different — harder, colder, or more open to judgement.

practice.whenToUse: Use when financial growth triggers the fear of becoming someone whose character is open to question, or when wealth and goodness feel mutually exclusive.

content.text: A daily affirmation for the fear that having more changes who you are. The affirmation names both things: the wealth and the character, held as compatible.
I can be wealthy and kind. My wealth belongs to me and doesn't change who I am.

Say it three times in the morning, hand on heart. Say it again when financial success triggers the fear of judgement — when having more feels like a risk to how you are seen or to who you are. The affirmation holds both things: the wealth and the person.
